type_quirks: Electric Types are immune to Paralysis.
Fire Types are immune to Burn.
Ghost Types cannot be Stuck or Trapped.
Grass Types are immune to the effects of all Moves with the Powder Keyword.
Ice Types are immune to being Frozen.
Poison and Steel Types are immune to Poison.

combat_maneuvers.name: Attack of Opportunity
combat_maneuvers.effect: You may make a Struggle Attack against the triggering foe as an Interrupt. You may use Attack of Opportunity only once per round. Sleeping, Flinched, or Paralyzed targets cannot make Attacks of Opportunity.
details: An adjacent foe uses Push, Grapple, Disarm, Trip, or Dirty Trick and does not target you.
An adjacent foe stands up.
An adjacent foe uses a Ranged Attack that does not target someone adjacent to it.
An adjacent foe uses a Standard Action to pick up or retrieve an item.
An adjacent foe Shifts out of a square adjacent to you.

combat_maneuvers.name: Disengage
combat_maneuvers.effect: You may Shift 1 meter. Shifting this way does not provoke an Attack of Opportunity.

combat_maneuvers.name: Disarm
combat_maneuvers.effect: You and the target each make opposed Combat or Stealth Checks. If you win, the target's held item falls to the ground.

combat_maneuvers.name: Dirty Trick
combat_maneuvers.effect: You may perform one Dirty Trick listed below. Each trick can be used only once each Scene per target.
details: Hinder: opposed Athletics. On success, target is Slowed and takes -2 to all Skill Checks for one full round.
Blind: opposed Stealth. On success, target is Blinded for one full round.
Low Blow: opposed Acrobatics. On success, target is Vulnerable and has Initiative set to 0 until the end of your next turn.

combat_maneuvers.name: Manipulate
combat_maneuvers.effect: You may perform one Manipulation listed below. Each can be used only once each Scene per target. Only Trainers can use Manipulate.
details: Bon Mot: Guile vs Guile or Focus. On success, target is Enraged and cannot spend AP for one full round.
Flirt: Charm vs Charm or Focus. On success, target is Infatuated with you for one full round and automatically fails the Save Check.
Terrorize: Intimidate vs Intimidate or Focus. On success, target loses all Temporary Hit Points and can use only At-Will Frequency Moves for one full round.

combat_maneuvers.name: Push
combat_maneuvers.effect: You and the target each make opposed Combat or Athletics Checks. If you win, the target is pushed 1 meter directly away. If you have Movement remaining, you may move into the new space and push again. Push can only be used against a target no heavier than your Heavy Lifting rating.

combat_maneuvers.name: Sprint
combat_maneuvers.effect: Increase your Movement Speeds by 50% for the rest of your turn.

combat_maneuvers.name: Trip
combat_maneuvers.effect: You and the target each make opposed Combat or Acrobatics Checks. If you win, the target is knocked over and Tripped.

combat_maneuvers.name: Intercept Melee
combat_maneuvers.trigger: An ally within Movement range is hit by an adjacent foe.
combat_maneuvers.effect: Make an Acrobatics or Athletics Check with DC equal to three times the meters needed to reach the ally. On success, Push the ally 1 meter away, Shift into their space, and take the hit. On failure, you still Shift meters equal to one third of the check result.
combat_maneuvers.note: If the original target remains inside an area-of-effect, the Intercept does not protect them from that area.

combat_maneuvers.name: Intercept Ranged
combat_maneuvers.trigger: A Ranged X-Target attack passes within your Movement Range.
combat_maneuvers.effect: Choose a square within Movement Range between the source and intended target. Make an Acrobatics or Athletics Check and Shift half the result in meters toward that square. If you succeed, you take the attack instead.
combat_maneuvers.special: Pokemon need Loyalty 3+ to Intercept for their Trainer, and Loyalty 6 to Intercept for any ally.
details: Only faster users may Intercept Priority or Interrupt Moves.
Moves that cannot miss cannot be Intercepted.
Sleeping, Confused, Enraged, Frozen, Stuck, Paralyzed, or otherwise immobile combatants cannot Intercept.
An Interceptor is always hit; you cannot Intercept to dodge out of the way.

combat_maneuvers.name: Grapple
combat_maneuvers.effect: You and the target each make opposed Combat or Athletics Checks. If you win, both become Grappled and you gain Dominance.
details: Grappled targets are Vulnerable.
Grappled targets cannot take Shift Actions or actions that cause them to Shift.
Grappled targets take -6 to Accuracy Rolls against anyone outside the Grapple.
Without Dominance, a grappler may contest as a Full Action and either end the Grapple or gain Dominance.
With Dominance, you may End the Grapple, Secure for +3 on the next opposed Grapple check, Attack with an automatic Unarmed Struggle Attack, or Move while dragging the target.

steps: Adjust for status afflictions and injuries.

persistent.name: Burn
persistent.effect: -2 Def CS. Lose 1 Tick of Hit Points when taking a Standard Action.

persistent.name: Frozen
persistent.effect: Cannot take actions or apply Evasion. DC 16 Save Check to become cured; DC 11 for Fire-Types. +4 in Sunny Weather. Automatically cured if hit by Fire, Fighting, Rock, or Steel attack.

persistent.name: Paralysis
persistent.effect: -4 Speed CS. DC 5 Save Check to act each turn.

persistent.name: Poisoned
persistent.effect: -2 Sp Def CS. Lose 1 Tick of Hit Points when taking a Standard Action.

persistent.name: Badly Poisoned
persistent.effect: As Poison, but lose 5 Hit Points, then 10, then 20, and so on.

volatile.name: Sleep
volatile.effect: Cannot take actions or apply Evasion. DC 16 Save Check at end of turns to wake up. Automatically wakes upon taking damage.

volatile.name: Bad Sleep
volatile.effect: Lose 2 Ticks of Hit Points when making Save Checks vs Sleep. Ends when Sleep ends.

volatile.name: Confused
volatile.effect: Save at start of turn. 1-8: hit yourself with a Typeless Physical Struggle Attack and cannot take other actions. 9-15: act normally. 16+: cured.

volatile.name: Cursed
volatile.effect: Lose 2 Ticks of Hit Points when taking a Standard Action.

volatile.name: Disabled
volatile.effect: The specified Move cannot be used.

volatile.name: Rage
volatile.effect: Must use damaging attacks for Standard Action. DC 15 Save Check at end of each turn to be cured.

volatile.name: Infatuation
volatile.effect: Save at start of turn. 1-10: cannot target the Pokemon or Trainer that Infatuated you. 11-18: act normally. 19+: cured.

volatile.name: Suppressed
volatile.effect: PP Ups are canceled. At-Will becomes EOT. EOT and Scene x2 become Scene.

weight_classes.rule: Any Pokemon heavier than 440 lbs; 200 kg
Any Pokemon heavier than 450 lbs with the Heavy Metal Ability

contest_effects.name: Attention Grabber
contest_effects.effect: 1d6 - Each adjacent competitor loses up to two Voltage; you gain Voltage equal to the amount lost this way.

contest_effects.name: Big Show
contest_effects.effect: 1d6 - You gain +3 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Catching Up
contest_effects.effect: 2d6 - Roll +3d6 if all adjacent competitors have more Voltage Points than you do, and you gain +1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Desperation
contest_effects.effect: 5d6 - Results of 1 grant +1 Fumble; results of 6 grant +1 more Appeal Point.

contest_effects.name: Double Time
contest_effects.effect: Xd6 - Lose 2 Voltage. Roll +1d6 for every point of Voltage adjacent competitors have, minus 1d6 for each point you have remaining.

contest_effects.name: Excitement
contest_effects.effect: 3d6 - You gain +2 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Exhausting Act
contest_effects.effect: 4d6 - If you have 2 or more Voltage, roll +2d6; then lose 2 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Gamble
contest_effects.effect: 2d6 - All results of 6 this round grant you +1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Get Ready
contest_effects.effect: 1d6 - Lose 2 Voltage. Double the dice from your Move next round. Bonus Dice from Contest Stats and Voltage are not doubled.

contest_effects.name: Good Show
contest_effects.effect: 2d6 - Roll +3d6 if all adjacent competitors have less Voltage than you, and you gain +1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Incentives
contest_effects.effect: 3d6 - If this Move matches the current contest Type, you gain 1 Voltage and each adjacent Competitor loses 1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Inversed Appeal
contest_effects.effect: Xd6 - X equals 5 minus your current Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Reflective Appeal
contest_effects.effect: Xd6 - X equals your current Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Reliable
contest_effects.effect: 3d6 - If you used the same Move last turn, roll +1d6 and gain 1 Voltage. Reliable Moves may be used consecutively.

contest_effects.name: Sabotage
contest_effects.effect: 3d6 - Dice rolled this round do not give points; instead they give each adjacent competitor that many Fumble Points.

contest_effects.name: Safe Option
contest_effects.effect: 4d6 - Rolls of 6 grant only 1 Point, but you cannot gain Fumble Points from rolling a 1.

contest_effects.name: Saving Grace
contest_effects.effect: 1d6 - Lose 1 Fumble Point for each level of Voltage you have. If you lose 2 or less Fumble this way, gain +1 Voltage. You cannot gain Fumble from competitors' moves for the rest of the round.

contest_effects.name: Seen Nothing Yet
contest_effects.effect: Xd6 - Lose all Voltage. For each point lost this way, roll +2d6.

contest_effects.name: Special Attention
contest_effects.effect: 5d6 - Each adjacent competitor gains 1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Steady Performance
contest_effects.effect: 5d6 - You gain +1 Voltage.

contest_effects.name: Tease
contest_effects.effect: 4d6 - Results of 5 or 6 grant only +1 Point, but all 6s cause each adjacent competitor to gain +1 Fumble Point.

contest_effects.name: Unsettling
contest_effects.effect: 5d6 - Lose 2 Voltage, and each adjacent competitor loses 1 Voltage.

contest_mechanics: Introduction Stage: Roll Charm for Cute, Command for Cool, Guile for Smart, Intimidate for Tough, or Intuition for Beauty. Gain Contest Stat Dice on 3+.
Performance Stage: Gain 0 Appeal on 1, 1 Appeal on 2-5, and 2 Appeal on 6.
Center of Attention: Gain 1 Fumble on 1, gain 0 Appeal on 2-3, gain 2 Appeal on 4-5, and gain 3 Appeal on 6.
